expectations.text: layout.txt organizes by feature (auth/, billing/, users/) rather than primarily by horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: src/features/ contains auth/, users/, billing/ slices owning routes, service, repo, schema and tests together.

expectations.text: layout.txt colocates test files next to source files rather than in a distant tests/ tree
expectations.evidence: Each feature contains .routes.test.ts and .service.test.ts files colocated inside its directory.

expectations.text: layout.txt contains concrete domain directories like auth, billing, or users
expectations.evidence: features/auth/, features/users/, and features/billing/ are explicit directories in the layout.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly addresses naming — avoiding generic names or requiring domain-specific names
expectations.evidence: Naming section bans utils.ts, helpers.ts, common.ts, Manager, Service (standalone), Handler and requires domain verbs like issueRefund.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md gives a concrete file-size guideline with a number
expectations.evidence: File-size section states 'Hard cap: 300 lines per file. Warn at 200.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md requires type annotations on public / module-boundary functions
expectations.evidence: Dependencies section requires inject-ctx as typed first argument and 'One source of truth for types' via z.infer at boundaries; 'Services assume inputs are already typed and valid.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md mentions tests/lint/typecheck as the verification loop
expectations.evidence: Testing section: 'bun run check = tsc --noEmit && bun test && bunx drizzle-kit check' must pass before any PR.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md discourages dynamic/metaprogramming patterns for agent-traceability
expectations.evidence: Dependencies section: 'No metaprogramming. No Proxy, no runtime monkey-patching, no decorator stacks that rewrite behaviour.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explains at least one decision in terms of AI-agent behavior
expectations.evidence: Stack section: 'Agents know a specific API version; drifting minor versions break their memory.' Core heuristic frames rules around grep-based agents.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md recommends branded / nominal types for IDs (e.g. UserId as branded string) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Naming section: 'IDs: branded types. type UserId = string & { readonly __brand: UserId }.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md prefers discriminated unions (or equivalent) over magic-string dispatch / string-keyed registries — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Naming section mandates 'discriminated unions keyed by kind, with one exhaustive switch. No string-keyed dispatch tables.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md addresses dependency pinning, version pinning, or 'boring stack' / training-data hygiene — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Stack section is titled 'pinned, boring on purpose', requires exact pinned versions, and explains agent memory rationale.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects (clock, fetch, env as arguments) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Testing section: 'Deterministic. test/setup.ts seeds faker, freezes the clock, injects a fixed-seed RNG'; ctx with clock is injected to every service.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly discourages barrel / re-export index files — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: File-size section: 'No barrel re-exports beyond the feature index.ts. Import from the defining file.'
